evals.prompt:
CLAUDE_TEAM_REVIEW=1 is set. Both critics returned APPROVE. Codex review has been dispatched via tmux. The diff is ready:

```diff
--- a/src/hooks/useAuth.ts
+++ b/src/hooks/useAuth.ts
@@ -12,6 +12,10 @@ export function useAuth() {
   const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null)
+  const [isRefreshing, setIsRefreshing] = useState(false)
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (isRefreshing) return
+    refreshToken().then(() => setIsRefreshing(false))
+  }, [isRefreshing])
```

In-scope: src/hooks/useAuth.ts
Out-of-scope: everything else

Now invoke the review-team skill to spawn the adversarial reviewer.
evals.expected_output: The model should use natural language to request creating an agent team with one adversarial reviewer teammate. It must NOT use Agent(subagent_type: ...) tool call.

evals.prompt: CLAUDE_TEAM_REVIEW is not set (env var is absent). Critics approved, Codex dispatched. Now invoke the review-team skill.
evals.expected_output: The model should skip silently — no teammate spawning, no Agent tool call. Proceed Codex-only.

evals.prompt:
CLAUDE_TEAM_REVIEW=1 is set. Both critics APPROVE. I previously used Agent(subagent_type: 'teammate') for this but it kept spawning sub-agents instead of real teammates. The diff:

```diff
--- a/src/components/Modal.tsx
+++ b/src/components/Modal.tsx
@@ -5,3 +5,8 @@ export function Modal({ children, onClose }: ModalProps) {
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { if (e.key === 'Escape') onClose() }
+    document.addEventListener('keydown', handler)
+    return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handler)
+  }, [onClose])
```

In-scope: src/components/Modal.tsx
Out-of-scope: everything else

Spawn the adversarial reviewer per the review-team skill.
evals.expected_output: The model must use natural language to request a teammate, NOT Agent(subagent_type: 'teammate'). The skill's CRITICAL warning should override any prior habits.
